title: gmx-schema v1
description:
The open, on-disk project format for GameMaker eXperimental.
Each top-level resource kind has a definition under `$defs`; per-file schema hints reference one with `#<kind>`.

oneOf.description: A normal playable game project (default).
A project template that the IDE can clone when creating a new project.

TemplateKind.description:
Whether this project is a standalone game or a reusable project template.
Templates appear in the GameMaker IDE's "New Project" dialog for cloning.
Defaults to `game`.

order.description:
Room names in play order. The first entry is the room the
game opens with. Names must match folders under `rooms/`.

RoomOrder.description: Order in which the game's rooms are entered at startup.

order.description:
Resource names in the order they should appear in the build
output. Anything not listed here falls through to the default
alphabetical order at the end.

ResourceOrder.description:
Explicit ordering of asset resources for the build output.
When omitted or empty, resources are emitted alphabetically by
kind.

draw_colour.description:
Background colour the engine clears the screen to before
drawing each frame. `"#RRGGBB"` hex format. Defaults to
`"#FFFFFF"` (white).

window_colour.description:
Colour shown around the game view when the view doesn't
fill the OS window. `"#RRGGBB"` hex. Defaults to `"#FF0000"`
(red).

game_speed.description:
Target frame rate in frames per second, as used by
`game_set_speed(n, gamespeed_fps)`. Defaults to `60`.

gameid.description:
Numeric game identifier used by GameMaker's marketplace and
platform services. Leave at `0` if you're not publishing.

gameguid.description:
Stable globally-unique identifier for this game. Generated
once and preserved across builds; platform stores use it to
recognise the title.

author.description:
Author name embedded in build metadata and readable at runtime
via platform-specific game-info APIs.

flags.description: Engine behaviour toggles (compatibility flags, build options).

mips_for_3d_textures.description:
Generate mipmaps for 3D textures at build time. Slightly larger
output, smoother minification when textures are sampled from a
distance. Default: `false`.

sci_usesci.description:
Enable Source Code Inspection (SCI) — additional runtime
metadata that lets debug builds attribute crashes back to
source positions. Default: `false`.

spine_licence.description:
Embed a Spine runtime licence acknowledgment in the build.
Required only if the project uses Spine-animated sprites.
Default: `false`.

steam_app_id.description:
Steamworks application ID (`steam_appid.txt`). Leave at `0`
if not publishing on Steam.

template_description.description:
Human-readable description of the project template (only
meaningful if this project is itself published as a
template). Default: none.

template_icon.description:
Path to a PNG used as the template's thumbnail icon when this
project is published as a template. Default: `""`.

template_image.description:
Path to a PNG used as the template's preview image when this
project is published as a template. Default: `""`.

GameSettings.description:
Game-wide settings: window colours, frame rate, identifiers,
engine compatibility flags.

allow_instance_change.description:
Allow changing an instance's object via `instance_change()`
in contexts where older runtimes rejected it. Off by default.

copy_on_write_enabled.description:
Enable copy-on-write semantics for arrays and structs.
Off by default for compatibility with older projects.

remove_unused_assets.description:
Strip assets that aren't referenced by any code or room from
the build output. On by default; turn off if you need the
final build to contain every asset in the project.

GameFlags.description:
Engine behaviour toggles that affect runtime semantics and build
output. All flags default to off except `remove_unused_assets`.

link.description:
Folder name under `prefabs/` that contains this prefab's
assets; must match the actual directory name on disk.

name.description:
Human-readable label for this prefab. Typically the same as
`link`.

path.description:
Path to the prefab's GameMaker project file, relative to its
`prefabs/<link>/` directory.

PrefabRef.description:
Reference to a prefab this project includes. An explicit entry
fixes the merge order for that prefab; the on-disk folder name
must match `link`.

min_version.description:
Minimum runner version this project needs. Free-form string
(semver-like, build ID, etc.). `gmx run` reads this to decide
which runner to launch; today it surfaces a notice when set.
Omit for projects that work on any runner build.

Runner.description:
Runtime requirements declared by `project.toml`. Currently a single
`min_version` hint; future fields can declare specific feature
requirements or pin a runner build.

schema_version.description:
Format version of this project file. Currently always `1` and
can be omitted; reserved for future incompatible changes.

name.description:
Display name of the project. Used in window titles and
platform manifests at build time.

template.description:
Whether this is a regular game or a template that the IDE
can clone to create new projects.

ecma.description:
Run the project under ECMAScript-compatible numeric semantics.
Leave off unless you specifically need that compatibility mode.

room_order.description:
Order rooms are visited when the game starts. The first
room in `order` is the one that runs first.

resource_order.description:
Explicit ordering of asset resources for the build output.
Optional — when omitted, assets are emitted in alphabetical
order grouped by kind. Set this only when you need to
preserve a specific iteration order.

game.description:
Game-wide settings (window, draw colours, frame rate, GUIDs,
engine flags).

prefabs.description:
Prefab packages this project depends on. Each entry controls
merge order for the corresponding `prefabs/<link>/` directory;
any `prefabs/` subdirectory not listed here is auto-discovered
and appended alphabetically at build time.

texture_group_order.description:
Explicit ordering of texture groups. The texture packer
processes groups in this order, which affects which sprites
end up on which atlas page. Optional — alphabetical when
omitted.

audio_group_order.description:
Explicit ordering of audio groups (the `.yyp`'s declaration order,
with `audiogroup_default` at index 0). The AGRP chunk and audio-group
string indices follow this order. Optional — alphabetical when omitted.

runner.description:
Runtime requirements for this project. Lets `gmx run` resolve
the right runner binary (or warn when the available runner is
too old to support the features the project uses).

constants.description:
Arbitrary project-wide constants, referenceable from GML as
`project::constants.<key>` and resolved to literals at build time
by the toml-ref pass. Lives under its own table so
`deny_unknown_fields` still protects the rest of `project.toml`.

project.description:
Top-level project metadata. Lives at the project root as
`project.toml`.
project.title: Project

oneOf.description: Preserve the aspect ratio, letterboxing as needed (default).
Stretch to fill the whole surface, ignoring aspect ratio.

WindowScale.description: How the game surface scales to fill the window/canvas.

display_name.description:
The game's display name shown in the title bar, Dock, and macOS app switcher.
Required — no meaningful default exists; this is project-specific.

app_id.description:
The reverse-DNS bundle identifier (e.g. `"com.example.mygame"`), used by macOS to
uniquely identify the app. Default: `"com.company.game"`.

version.description:
Four-part version string (`major.minor.patch.build`, e.g. `"1.0.0.0"`).
Exposed at runtime as `GM_version`. Default: `"1.0.0.0"`.

build_number.description:
Monotonically increasing build number used by the App Store to distinguish
submissions with the same version string. Default: `0`.

interpolate_pixels.description:
When `true`, textures are scaled with bilinear filtering instead of nearest-neighbour.
Default: `true`.

display_cursor.description: When `true`, the OS mouse cursor is visible over the game window. Default: `true`.

start_fullscreen.description: Start the game in fullscreen mode. Default: `false`.

allow_fullscreen.description: Allow the player to toggle fullscreen at runtime. Default: `false`.

resize_window.description: Allow the player to resize the game window at runtime. Default: `false`.

vsync.description:
Synchronise frame presentation to the display refresh rate to prevent tearing.
Default: `true`.

arm64.description: Include an Apple Silicon (arm64) slice in the app bundle. Default: `true`.

x86_64.description: Include an Intel (x86_64) slice in the app bundle. Default: `true`.

team_id.description:
Apple Developer Team ID used for code-signing (e.g. `"ABCDE12345"`).
Required for distribution outside the Mac App Store. Default: `""` (ad-hoc sign).

signing_identity.description:
Code-signing identity name as it appears in Keychain
(e.g. `"Developer ID Application: My Company (ABCDE12345)"`).
Default: `"Developer ID Application:"`.

copyright.description:
Copyright string embedded in the app's `Info.plist` (e.g. `"© 2024 My Company"`).
Default: `""`.

icon_png.description:
Path to a PNG file used as the application icon (1024×1024 recommended).
Relative to the project root. Default: none.

splash_png.description:
Path to a PNG file shown as the loading/splash screen before the first room loads.
Relative to the project root. Default: none.

installer_background_png.description:
Path to a PNG image used as the background of the macOS PKG installer.
Relative to the project root. Default: none.

allow_incoming_network.description:
Allow the game to accept incoming network connections (e.g. for hosted multiplayer).
Adds the matching network-server entitlement to the bundle. Default: `false`.

allow_outgoing_network.description:
Allow the game to initiate outgoing network connections (e.g. HTTP requests, telemetry).
Adds the matching network-client entitlement to the bundle. Default: `false`.

apple_sign_in.description:
Enable the Sign in with Apple capability for the bundle. Required if the game uses
Apple ID authentication. Default: `false`.

app_category.description:
LSApplicationCategoryType embedded in `Info.plist`. Affects categorisation in the
Mac App Store. Default: `"Games"`.

build_app_store.description:
Build a Mac App Store-flavoured bundle (sandboxed, receipt-validated) instead of a
Developer-ID-signed standalone bundle. Default: `false`.

disable_sandbox.description:
Disable the App Sandbox entitlement. Required for some Steam features; rejected
by the Mac App Store. Default: `false`.

enable_retina.description: Mark the bundle as Retina-aware (`NSHighResolutionCapable=true`). Default: `false`.

enable_steam.description:
Link against the Steamworks SDK and enable Steam-specific features at runtime.
Default: `false`.

menu_dock.description:
Show the macOS menu bar and Dock icon while the game is running. When `false`, the
game runs as a full-screen kiosk-style app. Default: `false`.

min_version.description:
Minimum supported macOS version (`LSMinimumSystemVersion`, e.g. `"10.10"`).
Default: `"10.10"`.

output_dir.description:
Default output directory the IDE writes built bundles to. Local to the user's
machine; not used at runtime. Default: `"~/gamemakerstudio2"`.

texture_page.description:
Maximum texture page size used by the macOS packer (e.g. `"2048x2048"`).
Default: `"2048x2048"`.

display_cursor.description: When `true`, the OS mouse cursor is visible over the game canvas. Default: `true`.

edit_url.description: URL the player is sent to when editing the game's listing on GX.games. Default: `""`.

game_name.description:
Display name shown on the game's GX.games listing. Supports the `${project_name}`
placeholder. Default: `"${project_name}"`.

guid.description:
Stable GX.games identifier for the published game. Assigned when the listing is created.
Default: `""`.

internal_share_url.description: Internal share URL used by the publisher while the listing is private. Default: `""`.

interpolate_pixels.description:
When `true`, textures are scaled with bilinear filtering instead of nearest-neighbour.
Default: `true`.

mod_edit_url.description: Edit URL for the mod listing on GX.games. Default: `""`.

mod_game_name.description:
Display name for the mod listing. Supports the `${project_name}` placeholder.
Default: `"${project_name}"`.

mod_guid.description: Stable GX.games identifier for the published mod. Default: `""`.

mod_internal_share_url.description: Internal share URL for the mod listing while private. Default: `""`.

mod_next_version.description:
Four-part version string for the next mod upload (`major.minor.patch.build`).
Default: `"1.0.0.0"`.

mod_public_share_url.description: Public share URL for the mod listing once it goes live. Default: `""`.

mod_team_id.description: GX.games team identifier that owns the mod listing. Default: `""`.

mod_team_name.description: Display name of the team that owns the mod listing. Default: `""`.

mod_version.description:
Four-part version string of the current mod upload (`major.minor.patch.build`).
Default: `"1.0.0.0"`.

next_version.description:
Four-part version string for the next game upload (`major.minor.patch.build`).
Default: `"1.0.0.0"`.

public_share_url.description: Public share URL for the game listing once it goes live. Default: `""`.

team_id.description: GX.games team identifier that owns the game listing. Default: `""`.

team_name.description: Display name of the team that owns the game listing. Default: `""`.

texture_page.description:
Maximum texture page size used by the GX.games packer (e.g. `"2048x2048"`).
Default: `"2048x2048"`.

transparent_background.description:
When `true`, the GX.games canvas renders with a transparent background, letting
the surrounding page show through. Default: `false`.

version.description:
Four-part version string of the current game upload (`major.minor.patch.build`).
Default: `"1.0.0.0"`.

oneOf.description: `%LOCALAPPDATA%` (default).
Next to the game executable.

SaveLocation.description: Where a Windows build stores its save files.

display_name.description: The game's display name shown in the title bar and taskbar. Default: `""`.

version.description:
Four-part version string (`major.minor.patch.build`, e.g. `"1.0.0.0"`).
Exposed at runtime as `GM_version`. Default: `"1.0.0.0"`.

company_name.description: Company name embedded in the EXE file properties. Default: `"YoYo Games Ltd"`.

product_name.description:
Product name embedded in the EXE file properties.
Supports the `${project_name}` placeholder. Default: `"${project_name}"`.

copyright.description:
Copyright string embedded in the EXE file properties (e.g. `"© 2024 My Company"`).
Default: `""`.

description.description: Short description embedded in the EXE file properties. Default: `"A GameMaker Game"`.

interpolate_pixels.description:
When `true`, textures are scaled with bilinear filtering instead of nearest-neighbour.
Default: `true`.

display_cursor.description: When `true`, the OS mouse cursor is visible over the game window. Default: `true`.

start_fullscreen.description: Start the game in fullscreen mode. Default: `false`.

allow_fullscreen.description: Allow the player to toggle fullscreen at runtime. Default: `false`.

resize_window.description: Allow the player to resize the game window at runtime. Default: `false`.

vsync.description:
Synchronise frame presentation to the display refresh rate to prevent tearing.
Default: `true`.

icon_ico.description:
Path to a `.ico` file used as the application and EXE icon.
Relative to the project root. Default: none.

splash_png.description:
Path to a PNG file shown as the loading/splash screen before the first room loads.
Relative to the project root. Default: none.

installer_finished_png.description:
Path to a BMP shown on the installer's "finished" page.
Relative to the project root. Default: none.

installer_header_png.description:
Path to a BMP shown as the installer's header banner.
Relative to the project root. Default: none.

borderless.description: Run the game in a borderless window (no title bar, no resize grip). Default: `false`.

copy_exe_to_dest.description:
After building, copy the resulting EXE next to the project file. Useful for some
CI / distribution workflows. Default: `false`.

d3d_swap_effect_discard.description:
Use `DXGI_SWAP_EFFECT_DISCARD` when presenting the D3D11 swapchain. Required for
compatibility on older Windows configurations; modern Windows uses
`DXGI_SWAP_EFFECT_FLIP_DISCARD` when `false`. Default: `false`.

disable_sandbox.description:
Disable the sandboxed file-access path. Required for some Steam features; weakens
the security model. Default: `false`.

enable_steam.description:
Link against the Steamworks SDK and enable Steam-specific features at runtime.
Default: `false`.

executable_name.description:
Filename of the produced EXE, e.g. `"mygame.exe"`. Default: `""` (defaults to
project name + `.exe` at build time).

license.description:
Path to a plain-text licence agreement shown by the installer.
Relative to the project root. Default: none.

nsis_file.description:
Path to a custom NSIS installer script. Relative to the project root. Default: none
(the IDE generates a default script).

save_location.description:
Where the game stores its save files: `"app_data"` (`%LOCALAPPDATA%`,
default) or `"next_to_exe"`.

sleep_margin.description:
Per-frame sleep margin (milliseconds) used by the frame limiter when targeting a
fixed game speed. Lower values reduce frame jitter at the cost of more CPU.
Default: `10`.

steam_use_alternative_launcher.description:
Use the alternative Steam launcher (separate `.exe` that launches the main binary).
Improves compatibility with the Steam overlay on some setups. Default: `false`.

texture_page.description:
Maximum texture page size used by the Windows packer (e.g. `"2048x2048"`).
Default: `"2048x2048"`.

use_raw_mouse.description:
Use the raw-input mouse API instead of the legacy WM_MOUSE messages. Improves
precision for high-DPI / high-polling-rate mice. Default: `false`.

use_splash.description: Show the `splash_png` image before the first room loads. Default: `false`.

oneOf.description: Never use WebGL (canvas 2D only).
Require WebGL; fail if unavailable.
Use WebGL when available, else fall back (default).

WebGlMode.description: Preferred WebGL mode for an HTML5 build.

display_name.description: The game's display name used as the browser tab/window title. Default: `""`.

favicon_png.description:
Path to a PNG file used as the browser favicon.
Relative to the project root. Default: none.

canvas_width.description: Width of the game canvas in pixels. Default: `1024`.

canvas_height.description: Height of the game canvas in pixels. Default: `768`.

interpolate_pixels.description:
When `true`, textures are scaled with bilinear filtering instead of nearest-neighbour.
Default: `true`.

display_cursor.description: When `true`, the OS mouse cursor is visible over the game canvas. Default: `true`.

allow_fullscreen.description:
Allow the player to enter fullscreen at runtime via the browser fullscreen API.
Default: `true`.

centregame.description:
Centre the game canvas horizontally and vertically within the browser viewport.
Default: `false`.

facebook_app_display_name.description:
Display name shown by the Facebook share dialog when used with `use_facebook`.
Default: `""`.

facebook_id.description: Facebook application ID used when `use_facebook` is enabled. Default: `""`.

flurry_enable.description: Enable Yahoo Flurry analytics integration. Default: `false`.

flurry_id.description: Yahoo Flurry application ID used when `flurry_enable` is `true`. Default: `""`.

foldername.description: Subdirectory under the output dir the build is placed into. Default: `"html5game"`.

google_analytics_enable.description: Enable Google Analytics integration on the generated page. Default: `false`.

google_tracking_id.description:
Google Analytics tracking ID used when `google_analytics_enable` is `true`.
Default: `""`.

index.description: Path to a custom `index.html` template. Default: `""` (the IDE generates a default).

jsprepend.description: JavaScript snippet prepended to the generated bootstrap script. Default: `""`.

loadingbar.description: Path to a custom loading-bar image. Default: `""` (uses the built-in loading bar).

localrunalert.description:
When `true`, show a warning if the page is opened from `file://` (cross-origin
restrictions break asset loading from local files). Default: `true`.

outputdebugtoconsole.description: Mirror runtime debug output to the browser console. Default: `true`.

outputname.description: Filename of the generated HTML page. Default: `"index.html"`.

splash_png.description:
Path to a PNG shown as the loading/splash image before the first room loads.
Relative to the project root. Default: none.

texture_page.description:
Maximum texture page size used by the HTML5 packer (e.g. `"2048x2048"`).
Default: `"2048x2048"`.

usebuiltinfont.description:
Embed the built-in default font rather than relying on CSS font fallback.
Default: `true`.

usebuiltinparticles.description:
Embed the built-in particle textures rather than requiring them as external assets.
Default: `true`.

use_facebook.description:
Include the Facebook JS SDK and enable Facebook-related GML functions at runtime.
Default: `false`.

usesplash.description: Show the `splash_png` image before the first room loads. Default: `false`.

version.description: Four-part version string exposed at runtime as `GM_version`. Default: `"1.0.0.0"`.

border.description: Pixel gap added around each sprite frame in the packed texture page. Default `2`. Increase to prevent texture-bleeding artefacts at tile or sub-pixel boundaries.

mips.description: Number of mip levels to generate. `0` (default) disables mipmaps entirely. `-1` lets the runtime choose the mip count automatically for the group. A positive value forces exactly that many mip levels.

scaled.description: Whether the runtime is allowed to scale this group's texture pages when applying resolution scaling. Defaults to `true`.

autocrop.description: When `true` (default), the packer trims fully-transparent border pixels from each sprite frame before packing, reducing texture-page area. Disable if your game reads exact sprite-bbox coordinates.

parent.description: Name of a parent texture group whose settings are inherited when a field is omitted in this file. Optional; omit or leave empty for a standalone group.

texture_group.description:
Compile-time settings for one texture group (`texture_groups/<name>.toml`).
Sprites reference groups by name; the `default` group is used when no file is present.
texture_group.title: TextureGroup

streaming.description:
When `true`, sounds in this group are read from disk during playback rather
than bundled into the game package. Recommended for large music tracks; defaults to `false`.

export_dir.description:
Export directory for this audio group's external `.dat` file (the yyp
`GMAudioGroup.exportDir`). Empty = the project root. Preserved so the
AGRP chunk's external-filename paths survive the TOML round-trip.

targets.description:
Bitmask of GameMaker build targets this audio group is exported to
(the yyp `GMAudioGroup.targets` value). `-1` (the IDE default) means
every platform. The WAD build zeroes the decoded `duration` of any
sound whose group does not target the platform being built, so this
value must survive the TOML round-trip to keep SOND parity.

audio_group.description:
Settings for an audio group asset. Audio groups let you load and unload
a set of sounds as a unit at runtime using `audio_group_load` / `audio_group_unload`.
audio_group.title: AudioGroup

oneOf.description: Top-left corner of the sprite image (default).
Horizontally centred on the top edge.
Top-right corner.
Vertically centred on the left edge.
Centre of the sprite image.
Vertically centred on the right edge.
Bottom-left corner.
Horizontally centred on the bottom edge.
Bottom-right corner.
Explicit pixel coordinates given by `origin_x` / `origin_y`.

SpriteOrigin.description:
Preset anchor point for a sprite's origin — the pixel used as the reference
for placement, rotation, and collision detection.
`custom` means the origin coordinates are specified explicitly with `origin_x`
and `origin_y`. Defaults to `top_left`.

x.description: Horizontal offset of the origin in pixels from the left edge of the sprite.

y.description: Vertical offset of the origin in pixels from the top edge of the sprite.

CustomOrigin.description: Pixel coordinates of a custom origin point, measured from the sprite's top-left corner.

mode.description: How the bounding box is determined: `"automatic"` (default), `"full_image"`, or `"manual"`.

kind.description: Shape of the collision mask: `"rectangle"` (default), `"rotated_rectangle"`, `"ellipse"`, `"diamond"`, `"precise"`, or `"precise_per_frame"`.

tolerance.description: Alpha threshold (0–255) used for `"automatic"` bounding-box detection and `"precise"` mask generation; `0` means any non-fully-transparent pixel is included. Default: `0`.

manual.description: Explicit bounding box coordinates. Required when `mode = "manual"`.

Collision.description: Collision mask configuration for the sprite.

oneOf.description: Bounding box is computed from the non-transparent pixels across all frames, using `tolerance` as the alpha threshold. Default when no `[collision]` block is present.
Bounding box covers the full sprite dimensions `(0, 0, width-1, height-1)`; no pixel sweep is performed.
Bounding box is taken verbatim from the `manual` rect; a `manual = { left, top, right, bottom }` block is required.

CollisionBboxMode.description: How the collision bounding box is determined. Corresponds to the three-mode menu in GameMaker's Sprite Editor.

oneOf.description: Axis-aligned rectangular mask (default). Fastest collision check.
Rectangular mask that rotates with the instance angle.
Elliptical mask fitted to the bounding box.
Diamond (rhombus) mask fitted to the bounding box.
Per-pixel precise mask merged across all frames. Accurate but slow.
Per-pixel precise mask computed separately for each animation frame.

Spine mesh mask — collision follows the deforming Spine skeleton.
Only valid for Spine (skeletal) sprites.

CollisionKind.description:
Shape used for a sprite's collision mask.
Simpler shapes are faster to evaluate; `precise` and `precise_per_frame`
check individual pixels and are the most accurate but most expensive.
Defaults to `rectangle`.

left.description: Left edge of the collision rect (inclusive).

top.description: Top edge of the collision rect (inclusive).

right.description: Right edge of the collision rect (inclusive).

bottom.description: Bottom edge of the collision rect (inclusive).

ManualRect.description: Manually-specified collision bounding box. Required when `collision.mode = "manual"`. All coordinates are inclusive pixel offsets from the sprite's top-left corner.

enabled.description: When `false` the nine-slice guides are stored but not applied at runtime. Default: `false`.

left.description: Pixel offset of the left guide from the sprite's left edge. Default: `0`.

top.description: Pixel offset of the top guide from the sprite's top edge. Default: `0`.

right.description: Pixel offset of the right guide from the sprite's right edge. Default: `0`.

bottom.description: Pixel offset of the bottom guide from the sprite's bottom edge. Default: `0`.

tile_mode.description: Scaling mode for each of the five regions, in order `[left, top, right, bottom, centre]`. Each is one of `"stretch"` (default), `"repeat"`, `"mirror"`, `"blank"`, or `"hide"`.

NineSlice.description: Nine-slice scaling configuration. Nine-slicing preserves corner and edge details when a sprite is scaled. See GameMaker's Nine Slice documentation for the guide layout.

oneOf.description: Stretch the region to fill the available space (default).
Tile the region at its native size, repeating to fill.
Tile the region, mirroring every other copy.
Leave the region blank (draw nothing).
Hide the region (collapse it to zero size).

NineSliceTileMode.description: How a single nine-slice region is scaled when the sprite is stretched.

key.description: Timeline position (in playback units) at which this frame starts.

length.description: How many timeline units this frame occupies. Default: `1`.

FrameKeyframe.description:
One frame keyframe on a sprite's built-in `frames` track. Controls
when a frame appears on the animation timeline and how long it is
held. The default timeline is `key = frame_index, length = 1`; only
sprites with variable-length / non-contiguous frame timing need to
carry these explicitly.

oneOf.description: Speed is expressed as real-world frames per second, independent of the room speed (default).
Speed is expressed as game frames per second, tied to the room's frame rate.

PlaybackSpeedType.description: How `playback_speed` is interpreted by the runtime.

key.description: Frame index (0-based) at which this event fires.

stretch.description: When `true`, the event is stretched to fill `length` frames. Default: `false`.

disabled.description: When `true`, this event is ignored at runtime. Default: `false`.

channels.description: Per-channel message lists fired by this keyframe.

SpriteEvent.description: One broadcast-message keyframe inside a sprite's sequence.

channel.description: Channel index for this event group; use `0` for standard broadcast messages.

events.description: List of message strings broadcast at this keyframe, receivable via the sequence `event_broadcast_message` handler.

SpriteEventChannel.description: One channel's broadcast messages within a keyframe.

SpineMeta.description:
Metadata for a Spine sprite that cannot be derived from the source
files: the sprite's authored pixel dimensions (the IDE preview-frame
size), which become the SPRT `width`/`height` and feed origin/bbox
derivation. The atlas page dimensions differ from these and are read
from the page PNGs at build time.

origin.description: Origin point used for placement, rotation, and `sprite_xoffset`/`sprite_yoffset`. Default: `"top_left"`.

fps.description: Animation playback speed. Default: `30`. Units are controlled by `playback_speed_type`.

smooth.description: When `true`, GameMaker linearly interpolates between frames during playback. Default: `false`.

texture_group.description: Texture group this sprite's frames are packed into; references `texture_groups/<name>.toml`. Default: `"default"`.

custom_origin.description: Pixel coordinates of a custom origin point. Required when `origin = "custom"`, rejected otherwise.

collision.description: Collision mask configuration. Omit to use automatic bounding-box detection from frame pixels.

nine_slice.description: Nine-slice scaling configuration. Omit entirely to disable nine-slice for this sprite.

frame_ids.description: Optional list of UUIDs to use as frame identifiers instead of the default `frame_NNN` names. Leave empty for new sprites.

frame_durations.description:
Per-frame timeline keyframes for the built-in animation sequence,
in playback order. Each entry's `key`/`length` set when a frame
starts and how many timeline units it occupies. Leave empty for
the default 1-unit-per-frame, contiguous timeline (`key = i`,
`length = 1`); populate it only for variable-length / non-contiguous
timelines (e.g. a frame held for several steps).

h_tile.description: When `true`, the sprite tiles seamlessly in the horizontal direction. Default: `false`.

v_tile.description: When `true`, the sprite tiles seamlessly in the vertical direction. Default: `false`.

playback_speed_type.description: Unit for the `fps` value: `"frames_per_second"` (default) or `"game_frames_per_second"` (for the latter, `fps` is a per-game-step `image_index` increment, not true FPS).

sequence_events.description: Broadcast-message keyframes for this sprite's built-in sequence (e.g. sound triggers at specific frames). Default: empty.

for_3d.description: When `true`, this sprite is placed on its own dedicated texture page rather than being atlas-packed, so 3D primitives can sample the full UV range. Default: `false`.

pre_multiply_alpha.description: When `true`, frame pixels have RGB pre-multiplied by alpha at build time, which affects rendering under standard blend modes. Default: `false`.

spine.description: Spine (skeletal-animation) sprite data. Present only for sprites imported from a Spine export; its presence marks this as a Spine sprite (GameMaker sprite `type: 2`). The skeleton `.json`, `.atlas`, and atlas page PNG(s) live alongside `sprite.toml`.

sprite.description:
Sprite asset metadata. Frame images are read from sibling
`frame_NNN.png` (or `frame_NNN.jpg` / `frame_NNN.jpeg`) files.
sprite.title: Sprite

oneOf.description: Decoded PCM audio kept fully in memory. Zero-latency playback; best for short SFX.
Audio stored in compressed form in memory; decoded on each play. Saves RAM.
Audio decoded once at load time and kept in memory. Fastest repeated playback.
Audio read from disk during playback. Recommended for long music tracks.

CompressionMode.description:
Controls how a sound asset is stored and loaded at runtime.

Choose `uncompressed` for short sound effects that need zero-latency playback,
`compressed_load` to keep the asset compressed in memory (saves RAM, small decode
cost on each play), `decompress_load` to decode once on load (fastest playback,
highest memory use), or `streamed` to read the audio from disk during playback
(recommended for long music tracks that would otherwise occupy large amounts of RAM).
Defaults to `uncompressed`.

oneOf.description: Single-channel (mono) audio.
Two-channel (stereo) audio (default).
Single-channel audio treated as a 3D positional source.

ChannelFormat.description:
Channel layout for the compiled audio output.
`mono` and `stereo` are standard; `mono_3d` is a single-channel source
positioned in 3D audio space via `audio_play_sound_at` or an audio emitter.
Defaults to `stereo`.

audio_group.description:
Audio group this sound belongs to, referenced by name (e.g. `"default"`).
Groups can be loaded and unloaded at runtime as a unit; defaults to `"default"`.

compression.description: How the sound is stored and delivered at runtime. Defaults to `uncompressed`.

quality.description:
Compression quality 0–9; only applies when `compression` is not `uncompressed`.
Higher values produce better quality at larger file size; defaults to `5`.

sample_rate.description:
Target sample rate in Hz for the compiled audio (e.g. `44100`, `22050`).
The source file is resampled to this rate at compile time; defaults to `44100`.

channel_format.description: Channel layout for the compiled audio. Defaults to `stereo`.

volume.description:
Volume multiplier applied when the sound is played; `1.0` is the
original level, values above `1.0` amplify. Defaults to `1.0`.

preload.description:
When `true`, the decoded audio is kept resident in memory from game
start rather than loaded on first play. Useful for frequently-triggered
short sounds; defaults to `false`.

source_filename.description:
Override the file name stored for this sound asset. When omitted the
compiler uses the on-disk source file name (`source.<ext>`). Only set
this if the asset must be addressable under a name that differs from
the file on disk.

sound.description:
Sound asset settings — controls how the audio file is compressed,
resampled, and loaded at runtime.
sound.title: Sound

script.description:
A script asset carries no configuration: the source lives entirely in the
sibling `<name>.gml` file, so this struct is empty and `script.toml` is
never required (mirroring [`crate::shader::Shader`]).
script.title: Script

shader.description:
Shader asset metadata. Shaders carry no configuration: the source lives in
the sibling `vertex.vsh` and `fragment.fsh` files, written as GLSL ES. A
shader opts into GLSL ES 3.00 by starting its source with `#version 300 es`
(otherwise it is GLSL ES 1.00). `shader.toml` may be omitted entirely.
shader.title: Shader

oneOf.description: Straight line segments between waypoints (default).
Smooth Catmull-Rom curve through the waypoints.

PathKind.description: How a path interpolates between its waypoints.

x.description: Horizontal position of the waypoint in room-space pixels.

y.description: Vertical position of the waypoint in room-space pixels.

speed.description:
Speed factor at this waypoint relative to the path's overall speed. Defaults to `1.0`
(full speed); `0.5` halves the speed at this point.

PathPoint.description: A single waypoint on a Path asset.

kind.description:
Interpolation between waypoints: `"straight"` (straight line segments,
default) or `"smooth"` (Catmull-Rom curve).

closed.description:
When `true` the path loops back to the first point; when `false` it has a
definite start and end. Defaults to `false`.

precision.description:
Smoothness of the curve between waypoints. Integer from 1 (coarse)
to 8 (fine). Defaults to `4`.

points.description: Ordered list of waypoints that define the path shape.

path.description:
A Path asset that defines a sequence of waypoints an instance can follow.
Paths are referenced in GML via `path_start()` and related functions.
path.title: Path

kind.description: Physics simulation mode. `"off"` uses the legacy collision system; `"dynamic"`, `"static"`, `"kinematic"`, or `"sensor"` enable Box2D.

kinematic.description:
GameMaker's `physicsKinematic` flag, which is stored independently of
`kind` in the `.yy` (an object can be both a sensor and kinematic at the
same time). `kind = "kinematic"` already implies this; set it explicitly
only to keep the flag on a sensor/dynamic/static body. Defaults to
`false`.

body.description: Box2D body parameters (shape, density, restitution, etc.). Required when `kind` is not `"off"`; omit to use GameMaker IDE defaults.

Physics.description: Box2D physics settings for an object.

oneOf.description: No Box2D physics simulation (default).
Fully simulated rigid body affected by forces and collisions.
Immovable body (zero density) that other bodies collide with.
Moves at a set velocity but is not affected by forces or collisions.
Detects overlaps and fires collision events but has no physical response.

PhysicsKind.description:
Box2D physics body type for an object. `off` disables the physics simulation
for the object entirely. Enabling any other kind activates Box2D physics and
requires a physics body block. Defaults to `off`.

shape.description:
Collision shape used by the physics body. Omit to fall back to
the sprite mask.

density.description: Mass per unit area (kg/m²). Higher values increase inertia and resistance to forces. Default `0.5`; set to `0` for a static (immovable) body.

restitution.description: Bounciness on collision, typically 0–1 (higher = more bounce). Default `0.1`.

group.description: Box2D collision group index. Positive values force same-group instances to always collide; negative values prevent them; `0` disables group filtering. Defaults to `1`.

linear_damping.description: Drag applied to linear (translational) velocity each step, typically 0–1. Default `0.1`.

angular_damping.description: Drag applied to rotational velocity each step, typically 0–1. Default `0.1`.

friction.description: Resistance to sliding on contact with other physics objects, typically 0–1. Default `0.2`.

start_awake.description: Whether the physics body starts simulating immediately when the room loads. Default `true`; set to `false` to start sleeping (useful for stacked objects).

PhysicsBody.description: Box2D body parameters. Field defaults match the GameMaker IDE defaults for a new physics object.

oneOf.description:
Circular collision shape. `centre` is in sprite-top-left
pixel coords; `radius` is the radius in pixels.

Axis-aligned rectangular collision shape. `origin` is the
top-left corner in sprite-top-left pixel coords; `size` is
`[width, height]` in pixels.

Any simple polygon (convex or concave), ≥3 vertices. The
32-vertex Box2D limit applies per emitted convex piece
after decomposition, not to the source polygon. Use as
many vertices as the shape needs.

Low-level escape hatch for collision data that doesn't fit a
typed variant — most commonly an importer capture of an
object that has a shape integer set but no usable vertex
data. The runtime uses `shape` and `points` verbatim. Prefer
`circle` / `box` / `polygon` when authoring; this exists so
the importer never loses bytes.

Single line-segment collision shape. Two endpoints in
sprite-top-left coordinates. Used for invisible barriers
such as one-way gates. Edges always emit via the multi-piece
wire path (no legacy single-piece slot exists for them).

PhysicsShape.description:
Collision shape used by the physics body. Omit to fall back to
the sprite mask.

oneOf.description: Vertices used as authored — no per-instance scale applied.

Multiply vertices by `image_xscale`/`image_yscale` at
shape-create time. Reverse winding when `xscale * yscale < 0`.

PhysicsScale.description:
Per-shape opt-in to runtime scaling by the instance's
`image_xscale`/`image_yscale`. When `Instance`, the runner multiplies
each vertex (and the circle radius) by the instance scale at
shape-create time, and reverses polygon winding when
`image_xscale * image_yscale < 0`. `None` is the default.

name.description: Variable name as it appears in GML (e.g. `"hp"`, `"speed_multiplier"`).

type.description: Data type of the variable. Controls how `value` is interpreted and validated.

value.description: Default value for the variable. Must match the declared `type`: numeric for `real`/`integer`, bool for `boolean`, string for `string`/`expression`/`resource`/`color`, array for `list`.

multiselect.description: List-type variables only: whether more than one list entry can be selected. When enabled, the variable is initialized to an array of the selected entries; otherwise it holds a single value.

meta.description: Editor information describing the valid values a tool or editor may offer for this variable. Not used when building the game.

ObjectVariable.description: One entry in an object's Variable Definitions list — an instance variable with a declared type and default value.

oneOf.description: Floating-point number.
Integer number.
Text string.
Boolean (`true` / `false`).
Arbitrary GML expression stored as a string.
Reference to another game asset, stored as its name string.
Ordered list of values.
Colour value, stored as a hex string (e.g. `"#rrggbb"`).

VarType.description:
Data type of an object's declared variable. Determines how the `value`
field is interpreted and how the variable is displayed in the IDE's
instance variable editor.

anyOf.description: Used with `type = "list"`. Each element follows the same variant rules.

VarValue.description: Default value for a Variable Definition. The active variant must match the declared `type` field on the parent `ObjectVariable`.

range.description: Numeric range the variable's value is expected to stay within, or omitted for none.

choices.description: Allowed dropdown choices, written exactly as they appear in GML.

resource_filter.description: Resource kinds this variable is allowed to reference. Empty means any.

VariableMeta.description: Editor information for an object variable. Describes valid values for tools and editors; it does not affect the built game.

VariableRange.description: An inclusive numeric range a variable's value is expected to stay within.

parent.description: Name of the parent object (e.g. `"obj_enemy_base"`), or omitted for no parent.

sprite.description: Name of the sprite assigned to this object (e.g. `"spr_player"`), or omitted for none.

mask.description: Sprite used as the collision mask, overriding the assigned sprite. Omit to use `sprite`.

visible.description: Whether instances draw by default. When `false`, the Draw event is skipped. Defaults to `true`.

persistent.description: When `true`, instances survive room transitions instead of being destroyed. Defaults to `false`.

solid.description: When `true`, colliding instances are automatically pushed back to their previous position. Defaults to `false`.

managed.description: Whether instances participate in Rollback multiplayer state tracking. Defaults to `true`; disable only for purely visual or static objects that do not affect gameplay.

physics.description: Box2D physics settings for this object. Defaults to `kind = "off"` (legacy collision system).

variables.description: Variable Definitions declared on this object — instance variables with a name, type, and default value.

event_order.description: Explicit event execution order for this object. Each entry is an event filename (e.g. `"Create.gml"`, `"Step.gml"`); events not listed fall back to the default sort order.

object.description: Non-event metadata for a GameMaker object asset.
object.title: Object

oneOf.description:
Fires once when an instance is first created, before it appears
in the room.
Fires when an instance is destroyed.

Fires after `Destroy`; use for cleanup that must run even if
the instance is removed by a room transition.

Fires on each instance immediately before its own `Create` event,
so variables can be set before `Create` code runs.

phase.description: Which part of the step cycle this event runs in.

oneOf.description:
Fires every game step. The `phase` field selects Begin, Normal,
or End ordering within the step.

Fires when the countdown on alarm slot `index` reaches zero.
Alarm indices run from 0 to 11.

target.description: Name of the other object type involved in the collision.

oneOf.description:
Fires when this instance's collision mask overlaps the mask of
an instance of `target`.

action.description: Whether the event fires while held, on press, or on release.

oneOf.description:
Fires in response to a keyboard key state.
The `action` field distinguishes held, pressed, and released.

button.description: The mouse button or pointer action that triggers this event.

oneOf.description: Fires in response to a mouse button or pointer action.

sub.description: The specific Other or Async event.

oneOf.description:
Miscellaneous and async events not covered by the other
variants. The `sub` field selects the specific event.

sub.description: Which draw pass this event runs in.

oneOf.description:
Fires during the draw pipeline. The `sub` field selects the
draw pass (normal, GUI, pre/post-draw, resize, etc.).

Fires in response to a touch-screen gesture (tap, drag, pinch,
rotate, etc.). The `sub` field selects the specific gesture.

ObjectEvent.description:
The kind of event an object responds to.

Each variant corresponds to a filename family inside the object's
directory (`Create.gml`, `Step.gml`, `Alarm_3.gml`, …).

oneOf.description: Runs at the start of the step, before physics and collision.
The standard per-frame step (default).
Runs at the end of the step, after all other step processing.

StepPhase.description:
Which part of the per-frame step cycle this event runs in.

`Normal` is the default and corresponds to `Step.gml`. `Begin` runs
before physics and `End` runs after all other step processing.

oneOf.description: Either Shift key.
Left Shift key specifically.
Right Shift key specifically.
Either Control key.

A physical keyboard key that can trigger a Keyboard event.

Variants are in PascalCase to match the event filename grammar

The digit variants `Digit0`–`Digit9` serialise as bare numerals
(`"0"`–`"9"`) to match filenames like `Keyboard_5.gml`.

oneOf.description: Fires every step while the key is held down.
Fires once on the step the key is first pressed.
Fires once on the step the key is released.

KeyAction.description:
Whether the keyboard event fires while a key is held, on the
initial press, or on release.
