kind.description: The kind of event (Create, Step, Alarm, Collision, etc.).

code_file.description: Path to the `.gml` file containing this event's code.

event.description: One event entry on an object.
event.title: Event

oneOf.description: No anti-aliasing — a crisp pixel font.
Anti-aliasing enabled (default).

FontAntiAlias.description: Whether a frozen font atlas was rasterised with anti-aliasing.

oneOf.description: ANSI charset (default).
DEFAULT charset.
SYMBOL charset.

FontCharset.description: Win32 charset recorded for a frozen font at rasterise time.

spread.description:
SDF spread in pixels — how far the distance field extends from each glyph edge.
Valid range 2–32; default `8`. Larger values allow wider shader effects but use
more atlas space.

SdfSettings.description:
Settings for Signed Distance Field (SDF) rasterisation.
SDF fonts scale without pixelation and support shader effects like glows and outlines.

from.description: First codepoint in the range (inclusive), e.g. `32` for space.

to.description: Last codepoint in the range (inclusive), e.g. `126` for `~`.

CharsetRange.description:
Inclusive Unicode codepoint range specifying which characters to rasterise.
Use multiple ranges to limit atlas size, e.g. `{ from = 32, to = 126 }` for
basic ASCII.

anyOf.description: A single family name, e.g. `"Arial"` or the generic `"sans-serif"`.

SystemFontFamily.description:
Typeface family: a single name string or an ordered list used as a fallback
chain. The compiler tries each entry in order and uses the first that is
installed on the build host.

font_name.description: Display name of the original typeface (informational only; not used for rasterisation).

size.description: Font size in points as set in the GameMaker Font Editor.

bold.description: Whether the bold variant was selected in the Font Editor. Default `false`.

italic.description: Whether the italic variant was selected in the Font Editor. Default `false`.

texture_group.description: Texture group the font atlas is assigned to. Default `"default"`.

ascender.description: Distance in pixels from the top of the glyph cell to the text baseline. Default `0`.

line_height.description: Total line height in pixels (ascender + descender + leading). Default `0`.

ascender_offset.description: Vertical offset in pixels applied to the ascender baseline. Default `0`.

anti_alias.description:
Anti-aliasing mode (GameMaker `AntiAlias`): `"on"` (default) or `"off"`
(a crisp pixel font). NOTE: SDF is **not** derived from this field — it is
signalled solely by a non-zero spread in the `[sdf]` table below
(matching GameMaker's `usesSDF`/`sdfSpread`). The compiler treats this
field purely as the AA mode.

charset.description:
Win32 charset code recorded when the font was created: `"ansi"` (default),
`"default"`, or `"symbol"`. Rarely needs to change.

sdf.description:
Signed Distance Field settings, present when the pre-baked `atlas.png` is
an SDF render (its alpha channel is a distance field, not coverage). The
runtime renders SDF fonts with the built-in `__yy_sdf_shader`; the spread
(`font_get_info().sdfSpread`) tells that shader the distance scale. Omit
for a standard anti-aliased atlas.

oneOf.description:
Pre-rasterised atlas (`atlas.png`) and glyph metrics (`glyphs.toml`)
live alongside `font.toml`. The compiler ships the atlas unchanged.

bold.description: Request the bold variant of the typeface. Default `false`.

italic.description: Request the italic variant of the typeface. Default `false`.

anti_alias.description: Enable anti-aliasing when rasterising glyphs. Default `true`.

texture_group.description: Texture group the font atlas is assigned to. Default `"default"`.

charset_ranges.description:
Unicode codepoint ranges to include as glyphs. If empty, the full
font character set is included.

sdf.description:
Enable Signed Distance Field rasterisation for resolution-independent
scaling and shader effects. Omit to use standard AA rasterisation.

oneOf.description:
Rasterises a bundled `source.ttf` (or `.otf`) at compile time.
Place the font file alongside `font.toml`.

family.description:
Typeface family to resolve. A single name (`"Helvetica"`) or an ordered
fallback chain (`["Helvetica", "Arial", "DejaVu Sans"]`) — the first
installed family wins. CSS generics `"sans-serif"`, `"serif"`, and
`"monospace"` are also accepted. Required.

bold.description: Request the bold variant of the typeface. Default `false`.

italic.description: Request the italic variant of the typeface. Default `false`.

anti_alias.description: Enable anti-aliasing when rasterising glyphs. Default `true`.

texture_group.description: Texture group the font atlas is assigned to. Default `"default"`.

charset_ranges.description:
Unicode codepoint ranges to include as glyphs. If empty, the full
font character set is included.

sdf.description:
Enable Signed Distance Field rasterisation for resolution-independent
scaling and shader effects. Omit to use standard AA rasterisation.

oneOf.description:
Resolves a host-installed typeface by family name and rasterises at
build time. No `source.ttf` sibling is required.

font.description:
Font configuration. Set `mode` to choose the rasterisation source:
`"frozen"` uses a pre-built atlas, `"ttf"` rasterises a bundled TTF,
and `"system"` resolves a host-installed typeface at build time.
font.title: Font

x.description: X position of the glyph on the atlas texture page in pixels.

y.description: Y position of the glyph on the atlas texture page in pixels.

w.description: Width of the glyph on the atlas texture page in pixels.

h.description: Height of the glyph on the atlas texture page in pixels.

shift.description: Horizontal advance (cursor shift) after drawing this glyph, in pixels.

offset.description: Horizontal draw offset from the cursor position to the glyph's left edge, in pixels.

Glyph.description: Metrics for a single glyph on the font atlas texture.

first.description: Unicode codepoint of the left glyph.

second.description: Unicode codepoint of the right glyph.

amount.description: Pixel adjustment to the cursor advance between `first` and `second` (negative = tighter).

KerningPair.description: A kerning adjustment between two adjacent glyphs.

glyphs.description: Map of character name (decimal codepoint string) to glyph metrics.

kerning.description: Kerning adjustment pairs for this font.

glyphs.description:
`fonts/<name>/glyphs.toml` — per-glyph metrics for a frozen font.
This file is generated automatically; manual edits are not supported.
glyphs.title: Glyphs

RoomSize.description: A width/height pair in pixels used for room and view dimensions.

visible.description: Whether this viewport is active at room start (default `false`).

x_view.description:
Left edge of the camera's view rectangle in the room, in pixels
(default `0`).

y_view.description:
Top edge of the camera's view rectangle in the room, in pixels
(default `0`).

w_view.description:
Width of the camera's view rectangle in the room, in pixels
(default `640`).

h_view.description:
Height of the camera's view rectangle in the room, in pixels
(default `480`).

x_port.description: Left edge of the viewport on screen, in pixels (default `0`).

y_port.description: Top edge of the viewport on screen, in pixels (default `0`).

w_port.description: Width of the viewport on screen, in pixels (default `640`).

h_port.description: Height of the viewport on screen, in pixels (default `480`).

h_border.description:
Horizontal follow border in pixels (default `32`). When
`object_follow` is set, the camera starts scrolling once the
target comes within this many pixels of the left or right edge.

v_border.description:
Vertical follow border in pixels (default `32`). When
`object_follow` is set, the camera starts scrolling once the
target comes within this many pixels of the top or bottom edge.

h_speed.description:
Maximum horizontal scroll speed in pixels per step when following
an object (default `-1` = instant snap). Has no effect when
`object_follow` is not set.

v_speed.description:
Maximum vertical scroll speed in pixels per step when following
an object (default `-1` = instant snap). Has no effect when
`object_follow` is not set.

object_follow.description:
Name of an object to keep centred in this viewport. The camera
follows the nearest instance of the named object, respecting the
border and speed settings above. Omit for a static camera.

RoomView.description:
One viewport entry for a room. A room supports up to 8 viewports (indices
0–7). Each viewport maps a rectangular region of the room (the camera
view) onto a rectangular area of the screen (the port).

flex_direction.description: Flex main-axis direction: `"row"` (default) or `"column"`. Layer only.

align_items.description:
Cross-axis alignment of children: `"auto"`, `"flex_start"`, `"center"`
(default), `"flex_end"`, `"stretch"`, `"baseline"`, `"space_between"`, or
`"space_around"`. Layer only.

justify_content.description:
Main-axis distribution of children: `"flex_start"`, `"center"` (default),
`"flex_end"`, `"space_between"`, `"space_around"`, or `"space_evenly"`.
Layer only.

flex_wrap.description:
Whether children wrap onto multiple lines: `"no_wrap"`, `"wrap"`, or
`"wrap_reverse"`. `None` when unset. Layer only.

RoomUiNode.description:
A node in a room's UI flex-layout tree. `kind` selects layer vs. flex
panel; the two carry slightly different layout fields.

oneOf.description: Lay children out horizontally (default).
Lay children out vertically.

FlexDirection.description: Flex main-axis direction for a room-UI layer.

oneOf.description: Inherit alignment from the parent.
Pack children at the start of the cross axis.
Centre children on the cross axis (default).
Pack children at the end of the cross axis.
Stretch children to fill the cross axis.
Align children along their text baselines.
Distribute children with space between them.
Distribute children with space around them.

AlignItems.description: Cross-axis alignment of children in a room-UI flex layer (Yoga `YGAlign`).

oneOf.description: Pack children at the start of the main axis.
Centre children on the main axis (default).
Pack children at the end of the main axis.
Distribute children with space between them.
Distribute children with space around them.
Distribute children with equal space around every child.

JustifyContent.description: Main-axis distribution of children in a room-UI flex layer (Yoga `YGJustify`).

oneOf.description: Keep all children on a single line (default).
Wrap children onto additional lines as needed.
Wrap children, reversing the cross-axis order.

FlexWrap.description: Whether a room-UI flex layer wraps its children onto multiple lines (Yoga `YGWrap`).

RoomUiFlexValue.description: A `{ value, unit }` dimension/spacing pair for room-UI layout fields.

oneOf.description: No unit (value is ignored).
Absolute pixels (default).
Percentage of the parent dimension.
Automatically sized.

FlexUnit.description: Unit for a room-UI flex dimension value (Yoga `YGUnit`).

size.description:
Width and height of the room in pixels. Omitting it deserializes to
the gx.games default of 1600×900 (the target most jammers ship to).

view.description:
Shorthand camera/view dimensions; omit to have the view fill the
whole room. For full per-viewport control use `views` instead.

enable_views.description:
Enable the room's viewport system (default `false`). When `true`
the viewports defined in `views` control what is drawn to screen.

clear_display_buffer.description:
Clear the display buffer before drawing each frame (default `true`).
Set to `false` for rooms that intentionally composite over the
previous room's image, such as transitions or overlays.

clear_view_background.description:
Clear each viewport's background with the room colour every frame
(default `false`). Independent of `clear_display_buffer`.

persistent.description:
When `true`, leaving and returning to this room preserves all
instance state instead of resetting to the room's initial layout
(default `false`). Persistent rooms use more memory.

creation_code.description:
Filename of this room's creation-code GML file, e.g.
`"RoomCreationCode.gml"`. Must be a bare filename ending in
`.gml` (no path separators, no `..`, not absolute) and the
file must exist next to `room.toml`. Omit the field if the
room has no creation code.

views.description:
Up to 8 viewport definitions for this room. Each entry maps a
rectangular region of the room (the camera view) to a position
on screen (the port). Omit to use GameMaker's default single
full-room viewport.

instance_order.description:
Explicit instance creation order (list of instance names). Controls
the sequence in which instances fire their creation events at room
start. Omit to use the default layer-declaration order.

physics_world.description:
Enable the Box2D physics world for this room (default `false`). Must
be `true` for any physics-enabled object instances to be simulated.

physics_gravity_x.description:
Horizontal component of the physics world's gravity vector in
pixels/s² (default `0`).

physics_gravity_y.description:
Vertical component of the physics world's gravity vector in
pixels/s² (default `10.0`, positive = downward).

physics_pix_to_meters.description:
Conversion ratio from pixels to metres used by the Box2D simulation
(default `0.1`, i.e. 10 px = 1 m).

ui_layers.description:
Room UI flex-layout tree (the IDE "UI Layers" panel, a `GMRoomUI`
resource). Each entry is a root `GMRUILayer`. Omit when the room has
no UI layers.

ui_name.description:
Resource name of the room's `GMRoomUI` (e.g. `"RoomUI"`). Preserved so
the synthesised resource entry/path round-trips. Omitted when the room
has no UI layers.

room.description:
Top-level metadata for a GameMaker room (size, views, physics, and
creation code). Layer contents live in sibling `layers/` files.
room.title: Room

sprite.description: Sprite resource ref. `None` = solid colour fill.

colour.description:
Solid-colour fill, or tint for a sprite background. Hex
`#RRGGBBAA` (alpha optional → opaque). `None` → no tint / no
fill (opaque white).

tiled_x.description: Tile the image horizontally across the room.

tiled_y.description: Tile the image vertically across the room.

stretch.description: Stretch the image to fill the room.

hspeed.description:
Auto-scroll horizontal speed in pixels per step. Combine with
`tiled_x = true` for a wrap-around scroll. Not camera parallax.

vspeed.description: Auto-scroll vertical speed in pixels per step.

fps.description: Animation FPS for sprite backgrounds. Defaults to `15.0` (GM IDE default).

x.description: Layer x offset; the background's start-of-tile position.

name.description: Parameter name, as defined by the effect.

EffectProperty.description:
One effect/filter parameter. Effects are GameMaker-defined; `kind`
is the parameter's type discriminator and `value` its stringified
representation.

id.description: Stable identifier for this placement; required.

scale_x.description: Horizontal scale; `1.0` = native size, negative flips.

scale_y.description: Vertical scale; `1.0` = native size, negative flips.

rotation.description: Rotation in degrees, counter-clockwise (0 = right, 90 = up).

creation_code.description:
Filename of this instance's creation-code GML file, e.g.
`"InstanceCreationCode_inst_42.gml"`. Must be a bare filename
ending in `.gml` (no path separators, no `..`, not absolute)
and the file must exist next to `room.toml`. Omit if this
instance has no creation code.

ignore.description:
IDE "ignore" flag — designer-only scenery instances kept in
the room's creation-order list but excluded from per-layer
instance lists at compile time.

image_index.description: Per-instance starting animation frame. IDE default 0.

name.description: Name of the object variable being overridden.

value.description:
Stringified value; the loader reparses against the object's
declared variable type. Accepts a native TOML scalar
(`value = 3.5` / `value = true`) or a string; stored canonically.

tile.description: Tile index into the layer's tileset.

TileLegendEntry.description:
One legend entry in an ASCII-encoded tile map. Each character maps
to a (tile-id, transform) pair.

rows.description: Block string of tile characters, one character per cell, rows separated by newlines. Width and height are derived from the first row length and line count.

anyOf.description: Compact text encoding: `rows` is a multiline string where each character identifies a tile via the parent layer's legend. Map dimensions are inferred from the string.

width.description: Width of the tile map in cells.

height.description: Height of the tile map in cells.

data.description: Row-major array of tile-index rows; each inner array must have `width` elements.

transforms.description: Per-tile transform overrides. Omit or leave empty if no tiles need flipping or rotation.

anyOf.description: Explicit encoding for maps that exceed the printable-ASCII legend limit. Each entry in `data` is one row of raw tile indices (0-based into the tileset).

TileMap.description: Tile-cell data for one tile layer. Choose `Ascii` for small maps whose legend fits in printable ASCII, or `Indices` when you need more than ~90 distinct tile cells.

tile.description: The tile index (within the tileset) that this transform applies to.

flip_x.description: Flip horizontally (mirrors the tile left-to-right). Corresponds to GameMaker's `tile_mirror` bit. Default `false`.

flip_y.description: Flip vertically (mirrors the tile top-to-bottom). Corresponds to GameMaker's `tile_flip` bit. Default `false`.

rotate.description: Rotation in degrees: `0`, `90`, `180`, or `270`. Applied after any flip. Default `0`.

TileTransform.description: Transform flags applied to a specific tile index in the `Indices` encoding. Absent tiles use no flip and `rotate = 0`.

id.description: Stable identifier for this placement; required.

scale_x.description: Horizontal scale; `1.0` = native size, negative flips.

scale_y.description: Vertical scale; `1.0` = native size, negative flips.

rotation.description: Rotation in degrees, counter-clockwise (0 = right, 90 = up).

id.description: Stable identifier for this placement; required.

scale_x.description: Horizontal scale; `1.0` = native size, negative flips.

scale_y.description: Vertical scale; `1.0` = native size, negative flips.

rotation.description: Rotation in degrees, counter-clockwise (0 = right, 90 = up).

sprite.description: Source-sprite name the tile crops from.

out_w.description: Output placed size in pixels (scales the cropped region).

ignore.description: Editor-only flag; ignored at compile time.

name.description: Optional stable tile name preserved from the imported project; leave unset for new tiles.

AssetTile.description:
One tile-shaped graphic placed in an `Assets` layer, with its own
source crop and output size.

id.description: Stable identifier for this placement; required.

ignore.description: Editor-only flag; ignored at compile time.

AssetText.description:
One Text element placed in an `Assets` layer (the IDE's "Text"
asset). Carried so a Text-bearing room round-trips through the
schema; the build re-emits it into the asset layer's `Texts[]` list.

oneOf.description: Align text to the left edge (default).
Centre text horizontally.
Align text to the right edge.

HAlign.description: Horizontal text alignment for asset-text elements (GameMaker `fa_*`).

oneOf.description: Align text to the top edge (default).
Centre text vertically.
Align text to the bottom edge.

VAlign.description: Vertical text alignment for asset-text elements (GameMaker `fa_*`).

depth.description: Render order: lower `depth` draws in front of higher.

effect.description:
Optional filter/effect ref applied to this background layer.
GameMaker lets any layer (not only the dedicated Effect
layer) carry an inline `effectType` + properties.

oneOf.description: A single-image background layer. The image source is a sprite.

depth.description: Render order: lower `depth` draws in front of higher.

effect.description:
Optional filter/effect ref applied to this instance layer.
GameMaker lets any layer (not only the dedicated Effect
layer) carry an inline `effectType` + properties.

oneOf.description: Instances placed in the room.

depth.description: Render order: lower `depth` draws in front of higher.

tileset.description:
Tileset resource ref. `None` when the layer has no tileset
assigned (`tilesetId: null` in the yyp); the ROOM chunk then
carries a tileset index of `-1`.

legend.description: Map character → tile cell (only for ASCII-encoded maps).

effect.description: Optional filter/effect ref applied to this tile layer.

x.description:
Layer x offset (GM `GMRTileLayer.x`). Compatibility tile layers
import with `x:10`; the ROOM chunk writes it as an f32 after depth.

oneOf.description: A grid of tile cells over a tileset.

depth.description: Render order: lower `depth` draws in front of higher.

tiles.description:
Individually placed tile-shaped graphics, each with its own
source crop and output size (distinct from a `tiles` layer,
which maps cells of a single tileset).

texts.description: Text elements placed in this asset layer.

oneOf.description: Asset / sequence / sprite layer (the IDE's "Asset Layer").

depth.description: Render order: lower `depth` draws in front of higher.

oneOf.description: Path-overlay layer (editor visualisation).

depth.description: Render order: lower `depth` draws in front of higher.

effect.description:
Effect identifier (e.g. `Bloom`, `Vignette`). The exact set
is GM-engine-defined.

effect_properties.description:
Per-effect parameters; names and value encodings are
GameMaker-defined.

oneOf.description: Effect / post-processing layer.

layer.description:
One room layer. The `kind` tag selects the layer type; `depth`
sets render order (lower `depth` draws in front).
layer.title: Layer

oneOf.description: Plays to the end then restarts from the beginning continuously (default).
Plays once from start to end, then stops.
Plays once from end to start, then stops.
Alternates direction each time the end or start is reached.

PlaybackMode.description:
Playback behaviour for a sequence asset.
Defaults to `loop`.

x.description: Horizontal origin in pixels from the left edge of the canvas.

y.description: Vertical origin in pixels from the top edge of the canvas.

Origin.description: Pixel origin of the sequence canvas — the reference point used for placement and rotation when the sequence is added to a room.

width.description:
Canvas width in pixels. GameMaker stores this as a float (the canvas
can have a sub-pixel size derived from its contents), so keep the
fractional value for byte-exact round-trips.

height.description: Canvas height in pixels (stored as a float, see `width`).

Size.description: Canvas dimensions of the sequence in pixels.

key.description: Start position of this keyframe on the timeline, in frames.

length.description: Duration of this keyframe in frames. Defaults to `1`.

stretch.description: When `true`, the keyframe content is stretched to fill its duration. Defaults to `false`.

disabled.description: When `true`, this keyframe is ignored during playback. Defaults to `false`.

channels.description: Per-channel event-name lists carried by this keyframe.

MessageEvent.description:
One keyframe in a sequence-level event store (`events` / `moments`). Fires
the named events on each channel at frame `key`.

channel.description: Channel index this event list belongs to. Defaults to `0`.

events.description: Ordered event names fired on this channel.

EventChannel.description:
One channel of a [`MessageEvent`] keyframe: the channel index plus the
ordered list of event names fired on it.

length.description: Total length of the sequence timeline, in frames. Defaults to `0`.

playback.description: How the sequence loops or plays once. Defaults to `loop`.

playback_speed.description: Playback speed value; units are controlled by `playback_speed_type`. Defaults to `60`.

playback_speed_type.description:
Whether `playback_speed` is measured in frames-per-second (`frames_per_second`) or
in game frames per second (`game_frames_per_second`). Defaults to `frames_per_second`.

volume.description: Master volume for all audio tracks in this sequence, `0.0`–`1.0`. Defaults to `1.0`.

origin.description: Pixel origin of the sequence canvas (the reference point for placement and rotation in a room). Defaults to `{x: 0, y: 0}`.

size.description: Canvas dimensions of the sequence in pixels. Defaults to `{width: 0, height: 0}`.

events.description:
Sequence-level broadcast-message events (`MessageEventKeyframe` store).
Each keyframe fires one or more named messages at a point on the
timeline. Defaults to empty.

moments.description:
Sequence-level moment events (`MomentsEventKeyframe` store). Each
keyframe runs one or more named moment scripts at a point on the
timeline. Defaults to empty.

sequence.description:
Top-level metadata for a sequence asset.
Set the canvas size, playback speed, loop mode, and origin here; add tracks in `tracks/`.
sequence.title: Sequence

key.description: Start position of this keyframe on the track timeline, in frames.

length.description: Duration of this keyframe in frames. Defaults to `1`.

stretch.description: When `true`, the keyframe content is stretched to fill its duration rather than cropped. Defaults to `false`.

disabled.description: When `true`, this keyframe is ignored during playback. Defaults to `false`.

channels.description: Per-channel data carried by this keyframe; each entry is one `KeyframeChannel`.

Keyframe.description: A single timed event on a track, placed at `key` frames into the sequence.

value.description: The numeric value at this keyframe.

anim_curve.description: Resource name of an animation curve asset to use for interpolation between keyframes.

embedded_curve.description: Inline animation curve definition embedded directly in this keyframe, instead of referencing an asset.

anyOf.description: Numeric value channel for `Real` tracks, with an optional animation curve.

colour.description: Colour value as a hex string: `#RRGGBB` or `#RRGGBBAA`.

anim_curve.description: Resource name of an animation curve asset to use for colour interpolation between keyframes.

embedded_curve.description: Inline animation curve definition embedded directly in this keyframe, instead of referencing an asset.

anyOf.description: Colour value channel for `Colour` tracks, with an optional animation curve.

asset.description: Resource name of the asset (object, sprite, or sound) active during this keyframe.

anyOf.description: Asset reference channel for `Instance`, `Graphic`, and `Audio` tracks.

wrap.description: Whether text wraps within the frame.

font.description: Resource name of the font asset to render with, if any.

anyOf.description:
Text payload channel for `Text` tracks. Must be the last variant: the
required `text` field disambiguates it from the other untagged variants.

KeyframeChannel.description:
The data payload for one channel of a keyframe.
Which variant applies depends on the parent track's kind:
`Real` tracks use `Value`, `Colour` tracks use `Colour`, and asset tracks (`Instance`/`Graphic`/`Audio`) use `Asset`.

channels.description:
One or more named channels that make up this curve. At least one channel
is required; each channel has its own interpolation function and control points.

AnimationCurve.description:
An animation curve asset — a named collection of channels, each defining
a piecewise function evaluated over `t ∈ [0, 1]`. Use in Sequences to
drive Real or Colour track parameters over time.

name.description:
Unique name for this channel within the curve (e.g. `"alpha"`, `"speed"`).
Used to retrieve the channel at runtime via `animcurve_get_channel`.

function.description:
Interpolation method used to compute values between control points.
`linear` (default), `smooth` (Catmull-Rom), or `bezier`.

points.description:
Ordered list of control points that define the curve shape. At least two
points are recommended; `x` values must be in ascending order within `[0, 1]`.

CurveChannel.description:
A single named channel within an animation curve, evaluated independently
from other channels. The channel produces one output value for any input `x ∈ [0, 1]`.

oneOf.description: Straight-line interpolation between points (default).
Smooth Catmull-Rom spline interpolation between points.
Cubic Bézier interpolation; tangent handles control the curve shape.

CurveFunction.description:
Interpolation method for an animation curve channel.
Determines how values are calculated between keyframe points.
Defaults to `linear`.

x.description: Horizontal position of the point along the time axis; must be in `[0, 1]`.

y.description: Output value of the curve at this point.

th0.description: Left-handle horizontal offset (tangent x, incoming) for Bézier interpolation; defaults to `0`.

th1.description: Right-handle horizontal offset (tangent x, outgoing) for Bézier interpolation; defaults to `0`.

tv0.description: Left-handle vertical offset (tangent y, incoming) for Bézier interpolation; defaults to `0`.

tv1.description: Right-handle vertical offset (tangent y, outgoing) for Bézier interpolation; defaults to `0`.

CurvePoint.description:
One control point on a curve channel. Bezier handles
(`th0`/`th1`/`tv0`/`tv1`) default to 0 and are only meaningful when
the channel's `function = "bezier"`.

oneOf.description: Audio gain (volume multiplier) for an `Audio` track.
Audio pitch multiplier for an `Audio` track.
Audio 3D falloff for an `Audio` track.
Rotation angle (degrees) of an instance or graphic.
Additive colour blend applied to an instance or graphic.
Multiplicative colour blend applied to an instance or graphic.
2D position (X/Y) of an instance or graphic on the canvas.
2D scale (X/Y) of an instance or graphic.
2D origin offset (X/Y) of an instance or graphic.
Playback speed multiplier for the sprite animation on a graphic track.
Sprite frame index for a graphic track.
Frame size for a text track.
Character spacing for a text track.
Line spacing for a text track.
Paragraph spacing for a text track.

BuiltinTrack.description:
Identifies which built-in GameMaker property a `Real` or `Colour` sub-track is animating.
Set this on sub-tracks of `Instance`, `Graphic`, or `Audio` tracks; freestanding tracks leave it `None`.
Two-channel properties (Position, Scale, Origin) encode X in channel 0 and Y in channel 1.

oneOf.description: No interpolation — the value steps to each keyframe.
Linear interpolation between keyframes (default).

Interpolation.description: How a track's value is interpolated between keyframes.

key.description: Start position of this keyframe on the track timeline, in frames.

length.description: Duration of this keyframe in frames. Defaults to `1`.

stretch.description: When `true`, the keyframe content is stretched to fill its duration. Defaults to `false`.

disabled.description: When `true`, this keyframe is ignored during playback. Defaults to `false`.

channels.description: Per-channel effect-parameter values, in GameMaker's serialized order.

AudioEffectKeyframe.description:
One keyframe of a [`Track::AudioEffect`] track. Each channel pairs an
explicit channel index (GameMaker may serialize them out of order) with the
effect parameter's current value at this point in time.

channel.description: Channel index this value belongs to. Defaults to `0`.

value.description: The effect parameter's current value at this keyframe.

AudioEffectChannel.description:
One channel of an [`AudioEffectKeyframe`]: the channel index plus the
effect parameter's current value.

name.description: Identifier for this track, unique within the sequence.

target.description: Resource name of the object asset to spawn.

is_creation_track.description: When `true`, this track is responsible for creating the parent asset instance.

sub_tracks.description: Parameter sub-tracks animating individual properties of this instance.

keyframes.description: Keyframes that determine which object instance is active at each point in time.

oneOf.description:
Places and animates an object instance on the sequence timeline.
Top-level keyframes identify which object to spawn; sub-tracks animate per-property curves
(position, scale, rotation, etc.).

name.description: Identifier for this track, unique within the sequence.

target.description: Resource name of the sprite asset to display.

sub_tracks.description: Parameter sub-tracks animating individual properties of this graphic (position, colour, etc.).

keyframes.description: Keyframes that determine which sprite is displayed at each point in time.

oneOf.description:
Displays and animates a sprite asset on the sequence canvas.
Top-level keyframes name the displayed sprite; sub-tracks carry per-axis curves.

name.description: Identifier for this track, unique within the sequence.

target.description: Resource name of the sound asset to play.

sub_tracks.description: Sub-tracks animating audio properties such as gain, pitch, or spatial position.

is_creation_track.description: When `true`, this track is responsible for creating the parent audio instance.

keyframes.description: Keyframes that control when the sound plays and for how long.

oneOf.description: Triggers or streams a sound asset at a point in the timeline.

name.description: Identifier for this track; for built-in properties this matches the GML property name.

builtin.description: Which built-in instance/graphic property this track drives, if any.

is_creation_track.description: When `true`, this track is the one that creates the parent instance or graphic resource.

keyframes.description: Keyframes carrying the numeric values (and optional animation-curve references) for this track.

oneOf.description:
Animates a single real-valued property (e.g. position, scale, rotation) over time.
Used as a sub-track of `Instance` or `Graphic`; `builtin` names the property being driven.

name.description: Identifier for this track; for built-in properties this matches the GML property name.

builtin.description: Which built-in colour property this track drives, if any.

is_creation_track.description: When `true`, this track is the one that creates the parent instance or graphic resource.

keyframes.description: Keyframes carrying the colour values (and optional animation-curve references) for this track.

oneOf.description:
Animates a colour-valued property (e.g. blend colour) over time.
Used as a sub-track of `Instance` or `Graphic`; `builtin` names the property being driven.

name.description: Identifier for this track, unique within the sequence.

target.description: Resource name of the nested sequence asset to play.

sub_tracks.description: Parameter sub-tracks animating individual properties of this nested sequence.

keyframes.description: Keyframes that determine which sequence is active at each point in time.

oneOf.description:
Plays a nested sub-sequence on the timeline.
Top-level keyframes name the referenced sequence; sub-tracks animate per-axis curves
(origin, position, rotation, scale).

name.description: Identifier for this track, unique within the sequence.

sub_tracks.description: Parameter sub-tracks animating text spacing and transform properties.

keyframes.description: Keyframes carrying the displayed text payload at each point in time.

oneOf.description:
Renders animated text on the sequence canvas.
Keyframes carry the text payload (string, font, wrap, alignment); sub-tracks animate
spacing and transform properties.

name.description: Identifier for this track (the effect's name, e.g. `audioEffect_peakeq`).

builtin_name.description:
Raw `eBuiltinTrack` tag identifying which audio effect this drives
(e.g. `43` = peak-EQ). Audio-effect tags are GameMaker-internal and
don't map onto the [`BuiltinTrack`] property enum, so keep the raw int.

is_creation_track.description: When `true`, this track is the one that creates the parent resource.

keyframes.description: Keyframes carrying each channel's effect-parameter current value.

oneOf.description:
Animates a single parameter of an audio effect (a `GMAudioEffectTrack`).
Used as a sub-track of an `Audio` track. Serializes byte-identically to a
`Real` track except for its model name; each keyframe channel's value is
the effect parameter's current value.

sequence_track.description:
One animation track inside a sequence. The `kind` field (the enum tag) selects the track type.
`Instance` and `Graphic` tracks may contain `sub_tracks` for per-property animation curves.

Note: `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` is intentionally absent — serde's internally-tagged enums
silently ignore that attribute.
sequence_track.title: Track

channels.description:
One or more named channels that make up this curve. At least one channel
is required; each channel has its own interpolation function and control points.

animation_curve.description:
An animation curve asset — a named collection of channels, each defining
a piecewise function evaluated over `t ∈ [0, 1]`. Use in Sequences to
drive Real or Colour track parameters over time.
animation_curve.title: AnimationCurve

draw_order.description: Draw order for particles within this system. Higher values are drawn on top of lower ones. Default: `0`.

particle_system.description: Particle system asset (`system.toml`). Controls system-wide draw settings.
particle_system.title: ParticleSystem

oneOf.description: Rectangular spawn region (default).
Elliptical spawn region.
Diamond-shaped spawn region.
Linear spawn region (a line segment).

ParticleShape.description:
Geometric shape of a particle emitter's spawn region.
Particles are created at positions within this shape.
Defaults to `rectangle`.

oneOf.description: Uniform distribution — equal probability across the shape (default).
Normal (bell-curve) distribution — particles cluster toward the centre.
Inverted normal distribution — particles cluster toward the edges.

ParticleDistribution.description:
Probability distribution used when placing new particles within the
emitter shape. Defaults to `linear`.

x.description: X coordinate of the left edge of the spawn region. Default: `0`.

y.description: Y coordinate of the top edge of the spawn region. Default: `0`.

w.description: Width of the spawn region in pixels. Default: `0`.

h.description: Height of the spawn region in pixels. Default: `0`.

Region.description: Axis-aligned bounding box that defines where the emitter spawns particles in the room.

max.description: Maximum time value (inclusive). A random value between `min` and `max` is chosen each use. Default: `0`.

TimedRange.description: A time range with explicit units, used for emission delay and interval.

oneOf.description: Time measured in game steps (frames). Value depends on the room speed.
Time measured in seconds, independent of room speed.

TimeUnits.description:
Time unit used for particle emitter timing values such as emission delay,
emission interval, and particle lifetime. Defaults to `frames`.

min.description: Minimum starting value; each particle picks a random value between `min` and `max` at spawn. Default: `0`.

increase.description: Amount added to the value every step over the particle's lifetime (negative = decrease). Default: `0`.

wiggle.description: Random amount added or subtracted each step (range -20 to 20). Default: `0`.

Range.description: A numeric range with optional per-step drift and random wiggle, used for speed, direction, lifetime, and size.

direction.description: Direction of the gravitational pull in degrees (0 = right, 90 = up, 180 = left, 270 = down). Default: `0`.

force.description: Strength of gravity added to particle speed per step (typically a small value such as `0.5`). Default: `0`.

GravityVector.description: Constant acceleration applied to every particle of this emitter each step.

min.description: Minimum starting angle in degrees. Default: `0`.

max.description: Maximum starting angle in degrees. Default: `0`.

increase.description: Degrees added to the angle every step (negative = counter-clockwise). Default: `0`.

wiggle.description: Random degrees added or subtracted each step (range -20 to 20). Default: `0`.

relative.description: When `true`, the angle is relative to the particle's direction of travel; if speed reaches 0 the direction resets to 0° and the sprite will jump. Default: `false`.

OrientationRange.description: Sprite rotation for particles, with optional per-step increase, wiggle, and direction-relative mode.

start.description: Colour at particle birth. Hex `#RRGGBB` or `#RRGGBBAA`. Default: empty (white).

mid.description: Colour at the midpoint of the particle's lifetime. Hex `#RRGGBB` or `#RRGGBBAA`. Default: empty (white).

end.description: Colour at particle death. Hex `#RRGGBB` or `#RRGGBBAA`. Default: empty (white).

ColourGradient.description: Three-stop colour gradient blended smoothly over each particle's lifetime (start → mid → end).

emitter.description: Name of the sibling emitter to trigger (the base name without the numeric prefix). Mutually exclusive with `preset`.

preset.description: Name of a built-in particle preset (a library emitter contributed by a forced prefab, e.g. `GM_Sparks`) to trigger instead of a sibling emitter. Mutually exclusive with `emitter`.

count.description: Number of particles to emit from the target emitter on each trigger. Default: `0`.

SubSpawn.description: Reference to a sibling emitter that should be triggered as a sub-spawn.

shape.description: Shape of the spawn region. Determines where within `region` new particles appear. Default: `rectangle`.

distribution.description: Probability distribution for placing new particles within the region shape. Default: `linear`.

sprite.description: Sprite asset name to use for particles. Omit (or `null`) to use the built-in `shape` pixel types instead.

sprite_animate.description: When `true`, the sprite animates across its frames over each particle's lifetime. Default: `false`.

sprite_stretch.description: When `true`, sprite animation is stretched to the particle's lifetime; otherwise it plays at the sprite's own speed. Default: `true`.

sprite_random.description: When `true`, each particle starts on a random sprite frame. Default: `false`.

texture.description:
Built-in particle texture index used when no custom `sprite` is set
(`GMPSEmitter.texture`). `7` is GameMaker's default round particle; `-1`
means no texture (e.g. an emitter that only triggers sub-spawn presets).
Ignored at build time when `sprite` is set (the sprite overrides it).

additive_blend.description: When `true`, particles are drawn with additive blending — overlapping particles brighten toward white, making dark colours nearly invisible. Default: `false`.

region.description: Bounding box of the spawn region in room coordinates.

emit_count.description:
Number of particles emitted per burst interval. Default: `0`.
`f32` to match GameMaker (`GMPSEmitter.emitCount` is a float and may be
negative).

emit_delay.description: Initial delay before the first emission, chosen randomly between `min` and `max`.

emit_interval.description: Time between consecutive bursts in stream mode, chosen randomly between `min` and `max`.

speed.description: Initial speed of each particle in pixels per step, with optional per-step increase and random wiggle.

direction.description: Initial direction of travel in degrees (0 = right, 90 = up, 180 = left, 270 = down), with optional per-step drift and wiggle.

gravity.description: Constant acceleration applied to every particle each step.

lifetime.description: Particle lifespan in steps (or seconds if `units` is `seconds`), chosen randomly between `min` and `max`.

size.description: Particle size as a scale multiplier relative to the sprite or built-in shape, with optional per-step growth and wiggle. Default multiplier `1.0` = original size.

orientation.description: Rotation of the particle sprite in degrees, with optional per-step increase and wiggle.

colours.description: Three-stop colour gradient applied over each particle's lifetime.

scale_x.description: Horizontal scale multiplier applied to the particle sprite/shape. Default: `1.0`.

scale_y.description: Vertical scale multiplier applied to the particle sprite/shape. Default: `1.0`.

spawn_on_death.description: Sub-emitter to trigger once when a particle reaches the end of its lifetime.

spawn_on_update.description: Sub-emitter to trigger every step for each live particle.

emitter.description:
One particle emitter — `emitters/<NN>_<name>.toml`.
The numeric prefix in the filename sets the emitter's evaluation order; the bare `<name>` is used by sub-spawn references.
emitter.title: Emitter

width.description: Width of one tile in pixels.

height.description: Height of one tile in pixels.

TileSize.description: Width and height of a single tile in the grid, in pixels.

h.description: Horizontal separation per tile side, in pixels. Defaults to `0`.

v.description: Vertical separation per tile side, in pixels. Defaults to `0`.

TileSeparation.description:
Pixel gap between tiles in the source sprite sheet.
Per `tileset_get_info`, the space between two adjacent tiles is `2 × separator` (one on each side).

x.description: Horizontal offset of the tile grid origin, in pixels. Defaults to `0`.

y.description: Vertical offset of the tile grid origin, in pixels. Defaults to `0`.

TileOffset.description: Pixel offset of the first tile from the top-left corner of the sprite sheet.

fps.description: Playback speed in frames per second.

frames.description: Ordered list of tile grid indices that make up the animation frames.

TileAnimation.description:
A named animation cycle: a sequence of tile indices played at a given frame rate.
Indices refer to positions in the tile grid (0 = top-left, left-to-right, top-to-bottom).

sprite.description:
Name of the sprite asset used as the tile sheet. `None` when the
tileset has no backing sprite (`spriteId: null` in the yyp); the
BGND entry then carries `sprite_index = -1` and packs no texture.

tile_size.description: Width and height of each tile, in pixels. Required.

separation.description: Gap between tiles in the source sheet. Defaults to zero on all sides.

offset.description: Pixel offset of the tile grid from the top-left of the source sheet. Defaults to zero.

output_columns.description: Number of tile columns in the packed atlas. Omit to let the compiler derive it from the sheet width.

output_border.description: Pixel border added around the tile atlas page. Defaults to `0`.

tile_count.description: Total number of tiles in the grid (rows × columns). Required for animated tilesets so the runtime knows the animation frame layout.

animation_speed.description: Playback speed for tile animations in frames per second. Defaults to `15.0`.

frame_data.description:
Flat list of tile indices describing every animation frame for every animated tile, in tile-index order.
Leave empty for a non-animated tileset; supply a flat sequence of `frame_count` indices per tile for animated tilesets.

animations.description: Optional named animation cycles for authoring convenience. Each entry names a subset of `frame_data` by tile index range.

sprite_no_export.description: When `true`, the backing sprite is excluded from the standalone sprite list and will not appear as an independent sprite asset at runtime; the tileset itself is still usable.

texture_group.description: Texture group that the tile atlas is packed into. Defaults to `"default"`.

step.description:
Frame (step) at which this moment fires, counting from the start of the
timeline. Moments are typically authored in ascending `step` order.

code.description: GML executed when the timeline reaches `step`. May be empty.

Moment.description: A single moment within a timeline: a frame number plus the GML run there.

moments.description:
Ordered moments that make up this timeline. May be empty (a blank
timeline). Each moment fires once when the timeline position reaches
its `step`.

timeline.description:
A timeline asset — a sequence of moments. Each moment runs its GML code
when the timeline reaches the moment's `step` (frame). Attach a timeline
to an instance with `timeline_index` + `timeline_running` to drive
scripted, frame-based behaviour.
timeline.title: Timeline
